findings.description: Comprehensive latency targets defined (NFR1-8, NFR35-39) but NO load test evidence exists. Targets: command submission <50ms p99, E2E lifecycle <200ms p99, event append <10ms p99, actor activation <50ms p99, ETag pre-check <5ms p99, query cache hit <10ms p99. All targets are code-documented but unvalidated.
evidence: PRD NFR1-NFR8: Detailed p99 targets defined
PRD NFR35-NFR39: Query pipeline p99 targets defined
NO k6, BenchmarkDotNet, or load test results found in repository
NO performance test scripts in .github/workflows/ or tests/
recommendations: CRITICAL: Implement k6 load test suite validating NFR1-NFR8 targets
CRITICAL: Add BenchmarkDotNet microbenchmarks for event append (NFR3) and actor activation (NFR4)
Add performance regression tests to CI pipeline (threshold-based pass/fail)

findings.description: Target: 100 cmd/sec/instance (NFR7), 1000 queries/sec (NFR39). Architecture supports horizontal scaling via DAPR actor placement, but throughput has never been validated under load. DAPR sidecar overhead target (<2ms p99, NFR8) is untested.
evidence: Stateless architecture theoretically supports horizontal scaling
recommendations: CRITICAL: Run k6 stress test with ramp-up to 100 cmd/sec per instance
Measure DAPR sidecar overhead in isolation
Test query pipeline at 1000 req/sec with ETag cache hit ratio measurement

findings.description: Kubernetes resource limits defined (EventStore: 250m-1000m CPU, 256-512Mi RAM; Sample: 100m-500m CPU, 128-256Mi RAM) but no profiling evidence under load. Actor memory consumption untested for 10K+ active aggregates (NFR17).
evidence: NO CPU profiling under sustained throughput
recommendations: Measure GC pressure during sustained command processing
Validate snapshot strategy prevents unbounded memory growth

findings.description: Caching architecture well-designed: ETag-based query caching with HTTP 304, self-routing ETag actors, projection change notification via SignalR, snapshot strategy for aggregate replay optimization (NFR19). DAPR resiliency policies provide connection pooling and retry optimization.
evidence: ETag actors for query cache (PRD FR50-FR60)

priority_actions: CRITICAL: Create k6 load test suite for NFR1-NFR8 validation (pre-GA blocker)
CRITICAL: Establish performance baselines with BenchmarkDotNet microbenchmarks
HIGH: Add performance regression gate to CI pipeline
MEDIUM: Profile resource usage under 10K active aggregates
summary: Performance posture is HIGH risk — not due to poor architecture (design is sound) but due to ZERO load test evidence for any defined target. All 13 performance NFRs (NFR1-8, NFR35-39) are untested. Architecture supports the targets but validation is completely absent. This is the #1 pre-GA risk.
